nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving or stagnant, soft, acidic water in marshes, streams, swamp forests, and lake margins; grows in saturated soil or submerged.

decorPreferences: planted in groups for a bushy effect

avoidWith: large herbivorous or vigorously digging fish (e.g., goldfish, cichlids that uproot plants)

summary: Mayaca Fluviatilis is a fine-leaved, feathery stem plant native to the Americas, from the southeastern United States to Argentina. Generally advised for keepers who can maintain soft, slightly acidic water and moderate lighting, it resembles a slimmer, finer-leaved Cabomba in form.
careNotes: Mayaca Fluviatilis is a delicate stem plant with extremely soft, flexible stems and narrow, bright green leaves that give it a graceful, feathery appearance — often compared to a slimmer, finer-leaved Cabomba. It is generally advised to keep this plant in soft, slightly acidic water around 24–26 °C, though it tolerates medium hardness reasonably well. The shoot tips are a useful indicator of iron availability: when iron runs low, new growth tends to fade to a very pale green, making a balanced trace-element fertilization routine helpful. Propagation is straightforward — trim healthy stems with several nodes and replant them directly into the substrate. Planted in dense groups, it forms the bushy, full look that makes it appealing as a background plant; sparse light or heavy pruning tends to produce leggy, open growth instead. In the wild, Mayaca fluviatilis grows across a wide range from the southeastern United States (Texas to North Carolina), through the Caribbean and Mexico, and south to Argentina, typically in slow-moving or still, soft water. Through the aquarium trade, it has become naturalized in parts of Asia. In hobby shops and online, it is commonly sold under the names Stream Bogmoss or simply Mayaca.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary care reference. Difficulty: medium; light medium; CO2 6-14 mg/L; height 15-30+ cm; soft slightly acidic water preferred but tolerates medium-hard.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked light, placement, propagation. Treats as easy-to-grow background stem; lower difficulty rating than Tropica.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirmed family Mayacaceae, pH 6.0-7.5, CO2 optional, max height ~5-12 inches (12-30 cm). Lists growth as slow vs Tropica's fast; medium is the averaged value.

additional.name: Aqualand Pets Plus
additional.notes: Used to triangulate temperature (20-28°C), gH (2-10 dGH), and max height (30-50 cm). Difficulty moderate, growth moderate - consistent with averaged intermediate.

additional.name: Wikipedia / USDA Forest Service
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Mayacaceae, order Poales) and native range (SE US to Argentina, naturalized in Asia).
